refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure teleoperation communication latency is within 150 ms p95 to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR specifies a high-level latency requirement for teleoperation safety. Refining it to a measurable latency threshold (p95 ≤ 150 ms) clarifies the performance target and safety relevance.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end communication latency under agreed network conditions using telemetry data during teleoperation sessions and verify p95 latency ≤ 150 ms.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication transmission delay over the network link.
FR2: Ensure processing and response time within teleoperation system components is within latency budget.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry feedback to the operator with minimal buffering delay.

candidate_dps: DP1: Use optimized network protocols and compression to reduce transmission delay.
DP2: Implement low-latency processing algorithms and hardware acceleration in teleoperation system.
DP3: Design telemetry data streaming with minimal buffering and prioritization.
selected_dp.selected_dp: Combination of DP1, DP2, and DP3 to holistically address latency across communication, processing, and telemetry feedback.

potential_sources: Tight coupling between telemetry streaming and teleoperation processing modules.
Dependency on network link quality affecting all DPs.
coupling_check.notes: The selected DPs are logically coupled but can be modularized with clear interface boundaries to minimize diffusion.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Risk of unexpected network congestion causing latency spikes.
Assumes hardware acceleration is feasible within vehicle and teleoperation modules.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Monitor network performance and consider fallback mechanisms if latency exceeds threshold.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Realizes teleoperation processing, telemetry streaming, and latency optimization logic.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Handles bidirectional communication between teleoperation module and vehicle.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Streams real-time telemetry data to operator interface.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives operator commands for vehicle control.

module_containment_scores.notes: High-level system module; contains Teleoperation but not focused on latency-specific logic.
Vehicle module processes commands but latency optimization logic is minimal here.
Primary module for latency-sensitive teleoperation logic; best containment and minimal diffusion.
Safety monitoring but not directly responsible for latency control.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Latency-critical teleoperation functions are best contained within the Teleoperation module to minimize diffusion and enable focused optimization.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Vehicle module for command execution timing.
Network Link variability may impact latency despite module containment.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Ensure clear interface contracts with Vehicle and Network Link modules to manage latency dependencies.

fr_dp_gate.reason: FR is well refined into measurable sub-FRs; selected DPs logically address each sub-FR with clear latency focus.

diffusion_risks: Some coupling between telemetry streaming and teleoperation processing could cause diffusion if not well modularized.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module shows strong containment scores and minimal diffusion risk for latency-critical functions.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency can be verified via telemetry and network monitoring tools within Teleoperation module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of latency-related functions to Teleoperation module with defined external interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Monitor network link variability and consider fallback or alert mechanisms in Safety Services.
Clarify interface timing contracts between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules.

decision.justification: Current level of decomposition and allocation meets requirements with clear verification paths and manageable risks; no further decomposition needed at this stage.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication transmission delay over the network link.
FR2: Ensure processing and response time within teleoperation system components is within latency budget.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry feedback to the operator with minimal buffering delay.

revise.why: No immediate revision required; proceed with implementation and verification planning.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Hardware acceleration is feasible in Teleoperation module.
evidence_needed: Latency measurements under operational network conditions.
Verification of processing times within Teleoperation module.
